subject: History
chapter: Chapter: When People Rebel – 1857 and After

key_points: The Revolt of 1857 was one of the first major rebellions against British rule in India
The revolt began in Meerut when Indian sepoys refused to use new rifle cartridges
Sepoys believed the cartridges were greased with cow and pig fat, which hurt religious beliefs
The rebels went to Delhi and declared Bahadur Shah Zafar as their leader
Important leaders of the revolt included Rani Lakshmibai, Nana Sahib, Kunwar Singh and Begum Hazrat Mahal
The revolt spread to places such as Delhi, Kanpur, Lucknow and Jhansi
The British finally suppressed the revolt using strong military forces
After the revolt, the British Crown took direct control of India and ended the rule of the East India Company
examples: Sepoys in Meerut refusing to use greased cartridges
Rani Lakshmibai fighting the British in Jhansi
Nana Sahib leading the revolt in Kanpur
Begum Hazrat Mahal organizing resistance in Lucknow
formulas: Start of revolt → Sepoy rebellion in Meerut → March to Delhi
Spread of revolt → Delhi + Kanpur + Lucknow + Jhansi
Result of revolt → End of East India Company rule → British Crown rule
exam_tips: Remember the year and starting place of the Revolt of 1857
Learn the names of important leaders of the revolt
Explain the main cause of the revolt (greased cartridges)
Mention the major change after the revolt (British Crown rule)
